project.name: portofolio
project.domain: Personal portfolio for a designer-minded creative developer
project.runtimeConstraint: Existing Vue 3, Vite, Tailwind, Lenis, GSAP. Add @fontsource-variable for Fraunces, Inter, JetBrains Mono. Drop @fontsource/bebas-neue and @fontsource/manrope.
project.architectureConstraint: Single-page portfolio composed as an exhibition catalog: cover, floor plan (index), room-per-project plate spreads, curator's about, colophon.

confirmedAssumptions: User issued a final decision sheet selecting Exhibition Catalog anchor (option C), Fraunces+Inter+JetBrains Mono triplet, OKLCH role-first 8-token system, 4-bucket motion charter without cinematic, dual-mode theme that is not an invert, plate-code unexplained-element move.
Photography is not yet captured. Interim renders use typeset plate panels (identification + chapter function) and the existing GitHub avatar in the hero only.
Custom cursor and grain overlay are removed. Lenis remains for smooth scroll only when reduced-motion is not requested.

discardedFromPrevious: Custom cursor and rule-line column wallpaper
regressionTestCriteria: Result must not read as the magazine issue with a different palette.
Hero composition is wide installation view + small wall label, not headline-stack with side proof.
Project list is a numbered floor plan, not a card grid or alternating spread variants.
Theme switch reveals two distinct moods (warm paper vs cool slate), not an inverted same image.

visualDirection.name: Exhibition Catalog
visualDirection.summary: A printed exhibition catalog where each project is a numbered room. Wall labels carry metadata, curator's notes carry argument, plate panels carry identity, colophon decodes craft.
visualDirection.firstViewportBet: A wide installation view dominates with a small wall label (year, role, plate code PR.MMYY) at lower-left. Headline set in Fraunces with opsz pushed near max so the hairline serif stays present.
distinctiveMoves: Photography (or typeset plate panel during interim) is the protagonist; copy supports the image rather than competing with it.
Hanging-punctuation global, tabular-num metadata, Fraunces opsz fixed at ~120 regardless of render size.
Plate code PR.MMYY appears unexplained on every room; only the colophon decodes it.

motionPaletteDecision.decision: Curatorial pacing, not cinematic. CSS animation-timeline: view() carries plate reveals; GSAP is restricted to the once-per-session opening sequence and theme micro-state. Default global easing cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.35, 1).
motionPaletteDecision.densityDecisionSource: User explicitly rejected scroll text reveal per word, parallax 3D, and custom cursor.
motionPaletteDecision.spatialDecision: Vertical scroll. No horizontal-only navigation. No WebGL. Composition uses three named CSS grid templates: plate-spread, index-floorplan, editor-note.

locked: Voice triplet: Fraunces author / Inter system / JetBrains Mono machine
Role-first OKLCH token system, 4 raw + 8 semantic
Dual mode that is not an invert (light hue 80, dark hue 250, accent hue 25)
Tri-state theme toggle (auto/light/dark) with no-flash inline script and color-scheme
No custom cursor, no decorative noise grain, no marquee tech logos
Plate code as the unexplained element decoded only in colophon
flexible: Exact OKLCH primitive values may shift to satisfy contrast or measured perceptual balance
Photography vs typeset plate panel ratio shifts as installation photos arrive
Plate-reveal implementation may swap to IntersectionObserver fallback if browser support data demands it
Lenis may be removed if dual-mode + reduced-motion cleanup proves cleaner without it

conceptualAnchor.name: Printed exhibition catalog
conceptualAnchor.anchorReference: Monographic show catalog: wall label, curator's note column, plate panel, floor plan, colophon
conceptualAnchor.specificReferencePoint: A small monographic exhibition catalog with numbered rooms (ROOM I, ROOM II), each opening on a wide installation view, narrow caption column, and plate code on the wall label.
conceptualAnchor.rationale: The anchor maps directly onto the existing data shape (summary as wall label, caseStudy as curator's note, productContext as deep dive) and earns the slow editorial pacing the user wants without copying any web portfolio.
conceptualAnchor.signatureMotion: Plate reveal via CSS animation-timeline: view() — clip-path inset opens left-to-right from entry 0% to cover 30%. Reads like a curtain pulling across a plate panel.
conceptualAnchor.typographicDecision: Fraunces variable carries author voice (headline, plate title, pull quote) at opsz ~120 regardless of render size; Inter variable carries system voice (body, nav, wall label); JetBrains Mono variable carries machine voice (plate code, year, stack tags) with tabular-num on.
conceptualAnchor.visualRiskBudget: High visual authorship through type, color discipline, and composition; no decorative chrome.
conceptualAnchor.motionRiskBudget: Single signature reveal plus one opening orchestration; everything else is micro feedback.

derivedTokenLogic.colorDerivationSource: Light surfaces derive from warm paper (hue 80) like a museum print catalog page; dark surfaces derive from cool slate (hue 250) like a dim gallery wall under spot lighting; accent terracotta (hue 25) is the single curatorial highlight, muted in dark.
derivedTokenLogic.spacingDerivationSource: Generous catalog margins, hairline rule lines as leader lines, narrow caption measures (38ch) to force a slow read.
derivedTokenLogic.typographyDerivationSource: Voice triplet Fraunces / Inter / JetBrains Mono; opsz lock on Fraunces titles; hanging-punctuation global; tabular-num on all metadata; text-wrap balance on display, pretty on body.
derivedTokenLogic.motionDerivationSource: CSS animation-timeline: view() for plate reveal; GSAP only for opening sequence and theme micro-state.
derivedTokenLogic.typeScaleMethod: fluid clamp() with role-distinct steps; display 32-120, body 16-18, mono 11-14
derivedTokenLogic.validationRule: Every motion and rule line must map to a catalog function (identification, leader line, plate reveal, focus handoff). If the rationale is decoration only, derive again.

aiSafeUiAudit.failureDefinition: An AI-safe portfolio would render a 3-column project grid with rounded cards, soft shadows, a centered hero tagline, generic Tailwind chrome, and a dark-mode invert.
aiSafeUiAudit.interchangeabilityTest: Renaming this site to a SaaS marketing page would break the wall-label metadata, plate code, floor plan numbering, curator's note, and colophon room. The anchor is product-specific.
requiredProductSpecificSignals: Wall label visible in the first viewport carrying year, role, plate code.
Project list is a numbered floor plan with row-per-room layout, never a card grid.
Plate code (PR.MMYY) appears unexplained throughout and is decoded only in the colophon.
aiSafeUiAudit.backgroundPatternRule: No grid wallpaper, calibration marks, scanlines, or noise. Hairline rules appear only as leader lines between wall label and caption.

productionContentPolicy.interimPhotographyHandling: Until installation photography is captured, rooms render typeset plate panels (large plate code + project monogram + year). The colophon explicitly states this interim state.

tokenSystem.sourceOfTruth: docs/design-intent.json -> colorTruth, plus src/style.css :root and [data-theme] blocks
tokenSystem.implementationNote: Primitives (paper, ink, slate, terracotta) defined once. Semantic roles (--surface-page, --surface-raised, --surface-sunken, --ink-primary, --ink-secondary, --ink-tertiary, --rule, --accent) switch by [data-theme]. Tailwind extend mirrors semantic names.

rawPrimitives.name: slate
rawPrimitives.purpose: cool dark surface base for dark mode

colorTruth.productSpecificBehavior: Light mode reads as a museum print catalog page in daylight. Dark mode reads as the same catalog under dim spot lighting. The shift in hue family (warm 80 vs cool 250) is intentional: the two modes carry different moods on purpose, not an inverted same image.

mutationRules.mobile: Single-column floor plan; plate panels stack; wall label moves above the photo; nav collapses to brand mark plus theme toggle.
mutationRules.tablet: Plate spread becomes 8/4; index floor plan compresses spacers; curator's note margin annotations remain inline.
mutationRules.desktop: Full asymmetric plate-spread (1fr 7fr 1fr 3fr), index-floorplan (1fr 1fr 6fr 1fr 3fr), editor-note (2fr 7fr 3fr) grids active.

standard.use: state change, panel open, theme switch

motionSystem.plateReveal: CSS animation-timeline: view() with clip-path: inset(0 100% 0 0) -> inset(0 0 0 0), entry 0% to cover 30%. Static end-state is the accessible default; motion is decorative timing only.
motionSystem.library: No new motion library. GSAP (already installed) is restricted to the once-per-session opening sequence (3 elements, <= 1.2s) and theme micro-state.
motionSystem.reducedMotion: All clip-path reveals and GSAP timelines disabled; static rest-state is the design.

rules: No 3-column tile grids for projects; floor plan rows only.
Plate panels look like typeset plate art, not framed thumbnails.
Repetition between rooms is intentional: the catalog grammar is consistent.

accessibilityPolicy.focusVisibleSpec: 2px solid oklch(--accent), 4px offset, in both themes; contrast >= 3:1 against adjacent surface verified.
accessibilityPolicy.themeToggleAnnouncement: aria-live polite region announces 'Auto theme', 'Light theme', or 'Dark theme'.

designExecutionPolicy.oldDesignRegressionTest: Result must not read as v2 magazine issue palette-swapped or as v1 dark cinematic. Plate spread, floor plan, and colophon are mandatory visual differentiators.

designExecutionHandoff.primaryExperienceGoal: Make the visitor walk through a catalog of rooms with the pacing of a curated exhibition: read wall label, look, read curator's note, move on. Decode plate codes only at the colophon.

surfacePlan.goal: Establish the protagonist with a wide installation view and a small wall label carrying year, role, and plate code.
State the design and product point of view in editor-note grid.
Show working method as four numbered method beats.
List rooms as numbered floor-plan rows with thumbnail-or-plate, title, and one-line summary.
Open each project on a wide plate (photo or typeset plate panel) with curator's note carrying constraint/decision/outcome.
Show timeline as numbered floor-plan rows with year-period and role.
Close the catalog with a contact action set in editor-note grid.
Decode plate codes, credit type and tools, state interim photography note.

libraryDecisions.purpose: Author voice (headline, plate title, curator's note opener) with opsz axis lock.
libraryDecisions.stableVersion: Latest variable subset; latin slice only.

libraryDecisions.purpose: System voice (body, nav, wall label, metadata).
libraryDecisions.stableVersion: Latest variable subset; latin slice only.
libraryDecisions.fallbackIfUnavailable: system sans stack (ui-sans-serif, system-ui, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif).

libraryDecisions.purpose: Machine voice (plate code, year, stack tags) with tnum feature on metadata blocks.
libraryDecisions.stableVersion: Latest variable subset; latin slice only.
libraryDecisions.fallbackIfUnavailable: system mono stack (ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace).

libraryDecisions.purpose: Plate reveal on entry; native scroll-driven animation.
libraryDecisions.fallbackIfUnavailable: Static end-state is the accessible default; no fallback choreography needed.

libraryDecisions.purpose: Once-per-session opening orchestration and theme micro-state.
libraryDecisions.fallbackIfUnavailable: CSS-only opening using transition + intersection observer or no animation.

libraryDecisions.purpose: Smooth scroll only when reduced-motion is not requested.

genericitySignals: project list rendered as a 3-column card grid
rounded-2xl + shadow-lg + gradient default Tailwind chrome
dark mode reads as inverted light mode
custom cursor or noise grain overlay reappears
scroll text reveal per word reappears
marquee of tech logos appears anywhere
validBoldSignals: wide installation view dominates first viewport with small wall label at lower-left
floor-plan numbered rows used for project list
plate code (PR.MMYY) appears unexplained on every room and is decoded only in the colophon
Fraunces opsz lock visible across renders
two distinct moods between light and dark (warm paper vs cool slate), not an invert

taintedSources: v2 editorial magazine palette and Bebas Neue
contextHygiene.approvedExternalConstraintUsage: Borrow only the conceptual frame of an exhibition catalog (wall label, curator's note, plate, floor plan, colophon). Do not copy any specific museum's brand.

forbiddenPatterns: centered minimal hero with one tagline
dark mode as inverted light mode
loader that fakes work without readiness gating

repoEvidence.designEvidenceSummary: Existing data shape (summary, caseStudy, productContext, impactMetrics, tags, year, link) maps directly onto exhibition-catalog tiers: wall label, curator's note, deep dive. Project entries already contain enough metadata for plate codes and floor-plan rows; commencement month is derived from project context and added as plateCode field.
